Column.description: Flex column container that stacks children vertically. Use for page layout, form sections, and any vertical arrangement of components.

children.description: Ordered list of child component IDs to render in this column

Row.description: Flex row container that arranges children horizontally. Use for side-by-side layouts, icon+text pairs, and toolbar-style arrangements.

children.description: Ordered list of child component IDs to render in this row

Divider.description: Visual separator line. Use to divide sections within a page.

Text.description: Text display component. Supports headings, body copy, and captions. Use 'variant' to control visual hierarchy.

text.description: Text content to display, or a data binding path (e.g. {"path": "/user/name"})

variant.description: Visual style variant. h1/h2/h3 for headings, body for standard text, caption for small secondary text

Icon.description: Renders a Material Symbols icon by name. Use for visual affordance alongside text or as standalone indicators.

Image.description: Image display component. Renders an image from a URL with configurable object-fit behavior.

url.description: Fully qualified URL of the image to display

fit.description: CSS object-fit behavior: 'cover' crops to fill space, 'contain' letterboxes, 'fill' stretches

GlassButton.description: Tappable button with multiple style variants. Triggers server-side actions or client-side navigation. Use 'filled' for primary actions, 'outlined' for secondary, 'text' for tertiary.

children.description: Child component IDs rendered inside the button (typically a Text or Icon)

variant.description: Visual style: 'filled' = solid background (primary CTA), 'outlined' = border only, 'text' = no border

action.description: Action to perform on tap. Use {name: string} for server events or {functionCall: {call: string, args: object}} for client-side navigation.

name.description: Server event name. The agent receives this event name when the button is tapped.

args.description: Arguments to pass to the function

GlassNavigator.description: Invisible tappable wrapper that makes any child component clickable. Use to make images, cards, or layout regions trigger navigation or actions.

child.description: ID of the single child component to wrap

target.description: Shorthand for navigation — surface ID to navigate to on tap

action.description: Action to perform on tap. Use {name: string} for server events or {functionCall: {call: string, args: object}} for client-side navigation.

name.description: Server event name sent when the child is tapped

GlassCarousel.description: Swipeable multi-page container with dot indicators. Use to present multiple screens or steps in a single mini-app. Each child is one full page.

children.description: Ordered list of child component IDs, one per carousel page

GlassHeader.description: Prominent page header with a title and optional subtitle. Typically placed at the top of a surface to give the page context.

subtitle.description: Secondary text displayed below the title

children.description: Optional action button component IDs (rendered right-aligned)

GlassListItem.description: List row component with leading avatar or icon, primary and secondary text, and optional trailing content. Use for contact lists, settings rows, feed items, and any repeated row patterns.

primary.description: Main label text for the list row

secondary.description: Smaller supporting text displayed below the primary label

icon.description: Material Symbols icon name shown as the leading element (use instead of 'avatar')

avatar.description: URL of an image displayed as a circular avatar (use instead of 'icon')

trailing.description: Short text shown at the trailing edge of the row (e.g. a timestamp, count, or status)

initials.description: Fallback initials when no icon or avatar is set

badgeVariant.description: Color variant for the trailing badge

chevron.description: Show a right-pointing chevron indicating tappable

action.description: Action to trigger when the row is tapped.

GlassBadge.description: Compact status indicator pill. Use inline with other components to show status, severity, or categorization at a glance.

text.description: Short label text to display inside the badge (e.g. 'Active', 'Failed', 'New')

GlassCard.description: Structured content card with optional hero image, title, subtitle, and badge. Use to present a discrete entity (product, person, place, event) with rich visual content. Supports arbitrary child components in its body.

children.description: Child component IDs rendered in the card body, below the title/subtitle

image.description: URL of a hero image displayed at the top of the card

title.description: Primary heading text for the card

subtitle.description: Secondary text displayed below the title

badge.description: Short label shown as a badge overlay on the card (e.g. 'New', 'Sale')

GlassInput.description: Single-line text input with a floating label. Supports data binding for pre-filling and reading values. Submitted form data is encrypted and relayed to the agent.

label.description: Floating label text shown above the input field

value.description: Initial value string or data binding path (e.g. {"path": "/form/name"})

inputType.description: HTML input type controlling keyboard mode and validation

placeholder.description: Placeholder text shown when the field is empty

hint.description: Helper text displayed below the field to guide the user

validationRegexp.description: Regular expression pattern for client-side validation

GlassTextarea.description: Multi-line text input for longer free-form responses. Use when the expected input is more than one sentence.

label.description: Floating label text shown above the textarea

value.description: Initial value string or data binding path

placeholder.description: Placeholder text shown when the field is empty

hint.description: Helper text displayed below the field

GlassSelect.description: Dropdown select input. Use when the user must choose one option from a predefined list.

label.description: Floating label shown above the dropdown

value.description: Currently selected option value or data binding path

GlassCheckbox.description: Checkbox input with a label and optional description. Use for binary opt-in/opt-out choices (e.g. 'I agree to terms').

label.description: Text label displayed next to the checkbox

value.description: Initial checked state (true/false) or data binding path

description.description: Optional explanatory text shown below the label

GlassSwitch.description: Toggle switch for binary on/off settings. Visually distinct from GlassCheckbox — prefer this for settings and feature toggles.

label.description: Text label describing what the switch controls

value.description: Initial toggle state (true=on, false=off) or data binding path

GlassRating.description: Star rating input. Use to collect a numeric rating from the user on a 1-to-N scale.

label.description: Label describing what is being rated

value.description: Initial rating value or data binding path

hint.description: Helper text displayed below the rating stars

GlassSlider.description: Range slider input for selecting a numeric value between min and max. Use for quantities, percentages, or any continuous range.

label.description: Label describing what the slider controls

value.description: Initial slider position or data binding path

min.description: Minimum value of the slider range

max.description: Maximum value of the slider range

suffix.description: Unit label appended to the displayed value (e.g. '%', 'kg', 'km')

hint.description: Helper text displayed below the slider

GlassStat.description: Single KPI metric display with an optional trend indicator. Use in dashboard layouts to highlight one important number. Supports grid spanning via 'span'.

value.description: The metric value to display prominently (e.g. '1,234', '$9.99', '42%')

label.description: Short description of what the metric measures (e.g. 'Monthly Revenue', 'Active Users')

trend.description: Direction of the trend arrow indicator

delta.description: Change value displayed alongside the trend arrow (e.g. '+12%', '-$4')

span.description: Number of grid columns this tile spans in a GlassBento layout

GlassGauge.description: Circular progress ring showing a value within a range. Use for capacity, completion percentage, or any bounded metric that benefits from a visual gauge.

value.description: Current numeric value to display on the gauge

min.description: Minimum value of the gauge range

max.description: Maximum value of the gauge range

label.description: Text label displayed below the gauge (e.g. 'CPU Usage')

suffix.description: Unit appended to the value inside the ring (e.g. '%', 'GB')

span.description: Number of grid columns this tile spans in a GlassBento layout

GlassBarChart.description: Horizontal bar chart for comparing values across categories. Use for rankings, comparisons, or any multi-item breakdown.

value.description: Numeric value determining the bar length

items.description: Data series for the chart, one object per bar

value.description: Numeric value determining the bar length

label.description: Chart title displayed above the bars

suffix.description: Unit appended to each value label (e.g. '%', 'ms', 'users')

GlassProgress.description: Horizontal progress bar for showing a single value as a proportion of its maximum. Use for task completion, loading states, or quota usage.

value.description: Current progress value (0–100 unless max is specified separately)

label.description: Text label describing what is being measured

suffix.description: Unit appended to the displayed value (e.g. '%', '/100')

GlassBento.description: CSS Grid bento-box layout container. Arrange GlassStat, GlassGauge, and other dashboard tiles in a responsive grid. Child tiles can span multiple columns using their 'span' property.

children.description: Ordered list of child component IDs to place in the bento grid

GlassCallout.description: Alert or notice banner. Use to draw attention to important information, warnings, errors, or confirmations. Not dismissible — always visible on the surface.

title.description: Optional bold title above the text

text.description: Message text displayed inside the callout

GlassMap.description: Embedded Google Maps view for a specific location. Use to show a place, venue, or address with optional metadata (distance, rating, hours).

latitude.description: Latitude of the map center point

longitude.description: Longitude of the map center point

label.description: Place name or title displayed on the map card

address.description: Street address displayed below the place name

distance.description: Distance from user shown as metadata (e.g. '1.2 km')

rating.description: Rating string shown as metadata (e.g. '4.5 stars')

hours.description: Opening hours shown as metadata (e.g. 'Open until 22:00')

GlassTimeline.description: Vertical timeline of ordered events or steps. Use for order tracking, process flows, activity history, or multi-step workflows.

label.description: Section heading displayed above the timeline

time.description: Timestamp or relative time label (e.g. '2 hours ago', '14:32')

status.description: Visual state: 'complete'=filled icon, 'active'=highlighted current step, 'pending'=future step

items.description: Ordered list of timeline events from top (oldest/first) to bottom (newest/last)

GlassTable.description: Data table with column headers and rows. Use for structured tabular data such as transaction history, comparison tables, or records lists.

label.description: Table title displayed above the table

columns.description: Ordered list of column header strings

items.description: One row: an array of cell values in the same order as 'columns'

rows.description: Table data — each element is an array of cell strings for one row

striped.description: Alternate row background colors for readability (default: false)

GlassCodeBlock.description: Syntax-highlighted code display. Use to show code snippets, configuration files, or any monospaced content that benefits from syntax coloring.

code.description: The code or text content to display

label.description: Optional caption or filename displayed above the code block
